items.title: Streamline runner by removing unnecessary flag in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the `run.ps1` script by eliminating the `-SkipBootstrap` flag, simplifying the boot process. The runner will now always execute the full flow, ensuring that new code changes are applied without the need for re-baking the image. This change enhances efficiency and maintains the public interface, while also keeping essential functions from previous updates.

items.title: Hotfixes oversized JWT token storage to restore admin logins in Backend 🚑
items.summary: This update fixes a production login outage caused by JWT access and refresh tokens exceeding the legacy 3000-character database limit after expanded permission codenames increased token size. The AuthToken model now stores tokens as TextField values, removing the artificial cap without changing API behavior or authentication flows. A lightweight Django migration updates the schema safely on PostgreSQL and prevents future regressions as permission counts grow.

items.title: Cleaned Up Broken AI Crawler References and Dead Assets in Docs 🧹
items.summary: Removed stale and fabricated entries from llms.txt and llms-full.txt after earlier content deletions left dozens of broken URLs behind. The update also deletes an unused api-key-bridge.js asset that was no longer referenced anywhere in the docs configuration. All remaining URLs were validated against existing .mdx files, bringing both crawler-facing indexes to zero broken links and improving documentation integrity for AI search tools and developers.

items.title: Completed the catalog-to-directory rename in Backend 🧹
items.summary: Renamed legacy catalog models, tables, modules, serializers, views, commands, and URLs to resource-typed Connector, Skill, and UserSkill names. Removed backward-compatible alias routes and serializer dual-fields, with a single reversible migration backfilling user skill sources from catalog to installed. Tests were updated around the new names and canonical paths, with obsolete alias and dual-field coverage removed.

items.title: Standardize Chinese Locale Folders in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update normalizes the Chinese locale folders to align with Mintlify's BCP-47 standards. The previous 'cn' folder is now 'zh-Hans', and 'zh-TW' has been renamed to 'zh-Hant'. All relevant content has been moved into their respective folders, ensuring consistency across the documentation.

items.title: Prepared the 3.35.3 mobile release rollout in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Updated Nexior to version 3.35.3 across package metadata and regenerated the lockfile for release consistency. The release shell now aligns iOS and Android version identifiers with the live 3.35.3 web bundle, preparing builds for TestFlight and the Play internal track. This keeps native release pipelines synchronized ahead of deployment.

items.title: Windows startup now finds preinstalled Python and skips unnecessary setup in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Updated the Windows runner to detect preinstalled Python from common install locations before attempting any downloads, preventing startup hangs on baked images. Added readiness checks for Wisdom and its dependencies plus a SkipBootstrap mode that can launch directly when the environment is already prepared. This speeds up autostart on pre-baked Windows images while preserving existing bootstrap behavior for fresh installations and development environments.

items.title: Enhance API Security with Token Requirement in Wisdom 🔒
items.summary: This update enforces API token authentication for all `/api/*` routes when the `API_TOKEN` environment variable is set. The implementation includes support for both Bearer token headers and query parameters, ensuring secure access to the API. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the authentication mechanism.

items.title: Enhance OTA Upload Speed with Global Acceleration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the COS global acceleration endpoint to improve upload speeds from GitHub Actions to the ap-beijing region. By routing uploads through Tencent's global edge, we significantly reduce timeout issues, enhancing the reliability of the publish-ota process. The necessary changes have been made to the client configuration and a new patch file has been added.

items.title: Refactor consent handling with new connector identifiers in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the consent handling to use the new `connector_identifier` and updates the related URL paths to `/connectors/`. Key changes include modifications to the `ConnectorConsentCard` and `connectorCatalogCache` to support both the new and legacy identifiers during the transition. The implementation ensures backward compatibility while preparing for future cleanup in AuthBackend.

items.title: Shared types updated for connector and skill identifier rollout in Common Frontend 🔄
items.summary: Shared frontend types now support the platform-wide catalog-to-connector/skill rename rollout by introducing canonical `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier` fields alongside deprecated `catalog_identifier` aliases. The update also refreshes connector catalog documentation to reference the new `/connectors/` API alias while preserving compatibility with legacy endpoints. These are type-only changes with no runtime behavior impact, allowing downstream clients to safely migrate during the staged backend rollout.

items.title: Connector & Skill Identifier Rollout Lands in Frontend Web 🔄
items.summary: Updated connection and skill operator types to adopt the new canonical `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier` fields while preserving backward compatibility with legacy `catalog_identifier` aliases. The Connections view now safely joins against either backend schema version, keeping connector metadata and permissions rendering stable throughout the staged rollout. Documentation comments and API references were also refreshed to align with the new `/connectors/` naming.

items.title: Connector Identifier Rollout Lands Across AI Chat Connection Flows in Platform Service 🔄
items.summary: This update completes the Stage 2 client-side migration from `catalog_identifier` to `connector_identifier` across AI chat connection handling, prompts, credential flows, and connector status checks. Matching logic now prioritizes the new field while preserving backward compatibility with legacy payloads during the rollout window. Tests were updated to validate both canonical and fallback paths, helping ensure safe deployment alongside the AuthBackend transition.

items.title: Refactor Connections: Rename Catalog Identifiers and Introduce New URL Aliases in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This PR marks the first stage of a refactor to eliminate 'catalog' references in the AuthBackend. Key changes include renaming `catalog_identifier` columns to `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier`, along with the introduction of new URL aliases for connectors and installed skills. Backwards compatibility is maintained through read-only aliases and dual-write mechanisms to ensure seamless integration for existing frontends.

items.title: Verified OpenAI MCP stays fully aligned with latest API docs in M C Ps ✅
items.summary: This update audited the openai MCP server against the latest OpenAPI documentation and confirmed full parity across supported endpoints, model enums, and request schema coverage. Checks covered chat completions, embeddings, image generation and edits, responses, and tasks APIs. No functional drift or missing schema mappings were identified, so no code changes were required.

items.title: Decoupling Docs from PlatformBackend & Revamping Structure in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully disconnects the Mintlify documentation site from the PlatformBackend sync, allowing for direct edits within the repository. It also introduces a restructured information architecture, enhancing user navigation with new tabs for Pricing, FAQ, and About, while adding several new pages to improve content accessibility and coherence.

items.title: Documentation sync for overview updates in M C Ps 📚
items.summary: This update addresses a sync issue triggered by changes flagged in the upstream documentation for the 'overview' section. Since there are no corresponding MCP servers or relevant entries in this monorepo, the changes are purely documentation-related, such as navigation adjustments. No code changes were necessary as the repository remains in sync.

items.title: Fix(mobile): add top safe-area padding so content clears iOS Dynamic Island / Android status bar in Nexior 🐛
Fix(ota): COS bucket region is ap-beijing, not ap-hongkong in Nexior 🐛
Fix(wisdom): emit cloudbase-init magic header so UserData actually runs in Backend 🐛
Ci(ota): publish-ota workflow + helper script for COS bundle releases in Nexior 🧪
Ship OTA live updates via Capgo with COS-hosted bundles in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Integrates @capgo/capacitor-updater to enable OTA delivery of JS/HTML/CSS bundles from a COS-hosted manifest, allowing hotfixes without app store releases. Adds a lightweight live update wrapper that runs fire-and-forget after the version gate to avoid blocking app startup. Updates Capacitor config, dependencies, and app bootstrap to support safe, feature-flagged update checks with rollback-safe behavior. Expands mobile release docs with a full OTA workflow and rollout guidance.

items.title: Implement Version Gate for Native Apps in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a version gate for the native iOS and Android applications, enhancing user experience during app startup. Depending on the installed version, users may encounter a hard block or a soft prompt to update, ensuring they are always using a supported version. The implementation includes new logic for version comparison and localized messages for various languages.

items.title: Documentation sync for overview updates in M C Ps 📚
items.summary: This update reflects a no-op sync triggered by upstream documentation changes for the `overview` service, including zh-CN translations and a DeepSeek navigation adjustment. As `overview` is a documentation-only service without an OpenAPI spec or corresponding MCP server, no code changes were necessary. The monorepo is now fully in sync with the latest documentation.

items.title: Enhance Suno Docs with Parameter Ranges in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the documentation for the Suno provider by clearly defining the valid ranges for the parameters `weirdness`, `style_influence`, and `audio_weight` as 0-1. The changes include updated descriptions in both English and Chinese documentation, as well as modifications to the OpenAPI schema to enforce these constraints. This ensures that SDK/UI integrators, including our Nexior frontend, can avoid validation errors when using these parameters.

items.title: Scale Advanced Sliders to 0-1 for Suno and Producer in Nexior 🎛️
items.summary: This update adjusts the advanced sliders for 'weirdness', 'style influence', and 'audio weight' in the Suno and Producer panels to a range of 0-1, aligning with the upstream API requirements. A migration script has been added to correct any persisted values greater than 1, ensuring a smooth transition for existing users. Additionally, all internationalization descriptions have been updated to reflect the new range.

items.title: New mobile app version endpoint added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new public endpoint, `GET /api/v1/app-version/`, which allows mobile clients to check for the latest and minimum supported app versions. This feature is crucial for managing client upgrades and ensuring compatibility with backend changes. The endpoint is unauthenticated and can be easily configured via environment variables.

items.title: Fix .env loading to ensure API_TOKEN availability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the configuration to load the `.env` file from the install root instead of the current working directory. This change ensures that the `API_TOKEN` is correctly read regardless of how the Wisdom application is launched, preventing bearer authentication from being disabled. This fix is crucial for the upcoming migration that renames the auto-provisioned key, enhancing security for tenant CVMs.

items.title: Update wisdom deployment and fix API token issue in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new migration that swaps the wisdom service deployment to the `img-eyraq5nz` image, which includes WeChat pre-installed and system Python 3.11. Additionally, the environment variable name has been corrected from `WISDOM_API_TOKEN` to `API_TOKEN`, resolving a silent authentication bug that affected bearer auth on tenant CVMs. These changes enhance the deployment process and ensure proper authentication functionality.

items.title: Enhance Windows Autostart for Seamless Boot in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Windows autostart command in `run.ps1` to trigger at system boot rather than user logon. By using `/SC ONSTART` and running as `NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM`, the task ensures that Wisdom starts automatically without requiring user login, enhancing deployment efficiency. Additionally, a `/Query` example has been included for easier task inspection.

items.title: Streamline Windows Deployment by Using System Python Directly in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the Windows deployment process by eliminating the creation of a virtual environment, which previously added unnecessary overhead. Now, dependencies are installed directly using the system Python 3.11, reducing boot time and simplifying the installation phase. The changes enhance efficiency and clarity for users running the deployment script.

items.title: Restore Python 3.8 Compatibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a future import to the auth module, ensuring compatibility with Python 3.8 by preventing the evaluation of PEP 604 union types at import time. The change addresses a critical issue that caused deployment failures and ensures that future rollouts of the auth-backend will proceed smoothly. All tests remain unaffected, maintaining stability in production.

items.title: Fix Windows PowerShell 5.1 parsing in deploy script and ASCII compatibility 🛠️ in Wisdom 🛠️
items.summary: This update resolves a PowerShell 5.1 parsing failure caused by Unicode dashes being misread under non-UTF-8 code pages. The script is now fully ASCII to ensure consistent parsing across Windows environments, including fresh installations. It also improves safety by renaming a shadowed automatic variable and adding a TLS 1.2 fallback for older .NET setups.

items.title: Unified OAuth Connection Naming Across Worker Flows in Platform Service 🔄
items.summary: Updated worker-side connection and auth-mode vocabulary to align with AuthBackend and AuthFrontend, replacing legacy values with oauth_dcr, oauth_static, byoc, and public. Implicit connection handling now routes through public, while MCP and OAuth logic were updated to recognize the new identifiers consistently. Test fixtures and validation coverage were refreshed to match the renamed models, with all TypeScript checks and test suites passing.

items.title: Align OAuth connection modes with AuthBackend vocabulary in Frontend Web 🔄
items.summary: Updated AuthFrontend to use the same shared connection-kind and auth-mode values as AuthBackend and aichat2-worker. The refactor replaces legacy values like `custom_oauth` and `oauth_preset` with `oauth_dcr`, `oauth_static`, `byoc`, and `public`, while removing `acedata_token` entirely. Connection filtering logic, inline auth handling, and related UI comments were refreshed to match the new platform-wide naming scheme. TypeScript and ESLint validation both completed cleanly.

items.title: Unified OAuth and Connection Modes Across Install Flows in Backend 🔄
items.summary: Standardized connection and connector auth enums around a shared vocabulary: oauth_dcr, oauth_static, byoc, and public, while removing the unused acedata_token path. Updated install flows, adapters, serializers, seeds, migrations, and tests to align with the new model and renamed the custom OAuth adapter stack to oauth_dcr. The migration safely remaps existing connection data and ensures downstream services can rely on consistent auth mode semantics during authorization and install handling.

items.title: Collapsed Windows Bootstrap Into One Re-runnable Script in Wisdom ⚡
items.summary: The Windows deployment flow was simplified into a single `run.ps1` entrypoint that installs Python if needed, downloads the latest release, preserves existing `.env` files, sets up dependencies, and launches Wisdom directly. Legacy bootstrap wrappers, scheduled-task registration logic, marker files, and README instructions were removed to reduce operational complexity. The new approach is deterministic and re-runnable, with autostart setup now handled separately through an explicit one-time `schtasks` command.

items.title: URL-Driven Auth App Gating Lands in Frontend Web 🚦
items.summary: Application Authorization UI access now uses the sticky `?features=authapp` URL flag instead of relying on backend config checks. The update wires both the application list and authorization views into the shared frontend feature-flag utility, matching the existing `sites` rollout behavior with cookie persistence and opt-in controls. Backend authorization enforcement remains unchanged, while the frontend gains safer beta rollout and rollback flexibility without requiring redeploys.

items.title: Windows bootstrap automation lands for custom image deployments in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Added a new Windows deployment bootstrap flow for custom images that downloads the latest Wisdom release from the CDN, installs it to C:\\wisdom, configures autostart, and launches the service automatically. The new PowerShell and batch bootstrap scripts support idempotent reruns, scheduled task self-healing, detailed bootstrap logging, and cloudbase-init integration. The flow also preserves externally provided .env configuration values, ensuring existing deployment token and environment precedence behavior remains intact.

items.title: Windows One-Click Release Setup Lands in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Added a full Windows-friendly install and launch flow for the Wisdom release zip, including new PowerShell scripts and double-clickable .bat shims for install, start, and stop operations. The release packaging workflow now ships all required scripts, configs, and helper files so end users can set up a local .venv and launch the server without manual CLI steps. README documentation was expanded with a guided quick-start flow for downloading, installing, and running Wisdom on Windows.

items.title: Application Authorization UI Opens Up for All Users in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The Application Authorization UI is now available to all users whenever the backend ENABLE_APPLICATION_AUTHORIZATION feature flag is enabled. This removes the previous superuser-only restriction so grantees can access the “Authorized to Me” tab and discover shared applications. Existing backend feature-flag gating and API-level access enforcement remain unchanged.
